channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: James Burrows, director of classic shows 'Cheers' and 'Friends,' dies at 85
item.description: Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.
item.content:encoded:
Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.

(Image credit: Jason LaVeris)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Writer David Sedaris says there's a radio drama that reminds him how lucky he is
item.description: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Writer David Sedaris talks about a radio drama that reminds him how lucky he is.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Writer David Sedaris talks about a radio drama that reminds him how lucky he is.

item.title: Algae clouded Trump's vision for the Reflecting Pool. But scientists aren't surprised
item.description: The shallow, sunny waters of the reflecting pool are an ideal incubator for algae growth in the summertime. Experts say the recent renovation may have helped accelerate it.
item.content:encoded:
The shallow, sunny waters of the reflecting pool are an ideal incubator for algae growth in the summertime. Experts say the recent renovation may have helped accelerate it.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: This Pride month, teen flicks are recasting familiar tropes with a queer sensibility
item.description: Teen movies like She's the He, Girls Like Girls and Leviticus are all turning tropes on their heads by centering queer characters.
item.content:encoded:
Teen movies like She's the He, Girls Like Girls and Leviticus are all turning tropes on their heads by centering queer characters.

(Image credit: NEON)
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Rhaenyra, Rhaena, Aegon, Aemond — let us help you keep up with 'House of the Dragon'
item.description: No one can blame you for getting lost in the fight over the Iron Throne. Here's our cheat sheet ahead of House of the Dragon's third season starting Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
House of the Dragon Season 3.'/>

No one can blame you for getting lost in the fight over the Iron Throne. Here's our cheat sheet ahead of House of the Dragon's third season starting Sunday.

(Image credit: Nye Caple)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Can you taste history? We try George Washington's original beer
item.description: Hops, yeast...and a lot of molasses
item.content:encoded:
Hops, yeast...and a lot of molasses

(Image credit: Jonathan Blanc/NYPL)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: How the 1874 Freedman's Bank collapse connects to economic disparities we see today
item.description: In Savings and Trust, historian Justene Hill Edwards tells the story of the Freedman's Bank, which was created for formerly enslaved people following the Civil War. Originally broadcast Nov. 7, 2024.
item.content:encoded: In Savings and Trust, historian Justene Hill Edwards tells the story of the Freedman's Bank, which was created for formerly enslaved people following the Civil War. Originally broadcast Nov. 7, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: 'Diaphanous' crowned the most beautiful English word
item.description: As part of a collaboration between the Poetry Society of America and Babbel, 1,500 poets and poetry lovers weighed in on the most beautiful word in the English language, crowning "diaphanous."
item.content:encoded: As part of a collaboration between the Poetry Society of America and Babbel, 1,500 poets and poetry lovers weighed in on the most beautiful word in the English language, crowning "diaphanous."
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: To Detroiters, Motown is more than just music
item.description: The story of Motown Records is a quintessentially American story about the American Dream, civil rights and entrepreneurship — but it has a special significance in Detroit, where it all started.
item.content:encoded: The story of Motown Records is a quintessentially American story about the American Dream, civil rights and entrepreneurship — but it has a special significance in Detroit, where it all started.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: 'Toy Story 5' surges into the digital age -- while also peering lovingly backward
item.description: In this delightful sequel, Jessie the cowgirl teams up with Buzz Lightyear and Woody to fend off the rise of digital devices, which are taking over the minds and attention spans of kids everywhere.
item.content:encoded:
In this delightful sequel, Jessie the cowgirl teams up with Buzz Lightyear and Woody to fend off the rise of digital devices, which are taking over the minds and attention spans of kids everywhere.

(Image credit: Disney)
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang
